name: Savory Pork and Vegetable Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 lb pork shoulder, lean, trimmed of fat
1 lb red potatoes, cut into 1-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim pork of all visible fat and then cut in 1-inch pieces.
2. Sprinkle pork with 1/4 tsp of the salt and pepper. Toss with the flour until coated.
3. Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a 5-quart pot over medium heat. Shake excess flour from pork and add to pan. Cook until browned, turning pork often, about 3 minutes. Remove to a plate. Repeat with remaining oil and pork.
4. Reduce heat to medium and add onions to sauté pan. Sauté until softened and light brown, about 5 minutes. Stir in garlic and cook for 1 minute.
5. Add pork, thyme, rosemary, apple cider vinegar, remaining 1/2 tsp salt, and one cup of water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until pork is just tender.
6. Add carrots, red potatoes, turnips, and the remaining 1 cup water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer until vegetables are tender. Remove 1 cup vegetables with broth. Puree in a food processor or blender with mustard. Gently stir into stew.
Linda's Crockpot Directions:
(Brown meat and sauté onions and garlic in oil as directed.) While these are browning, prepare the vegetables and put them in the bottom of the crockpot. Add the browned onions and garlic, then the meat (on top). Sprinkle seasonings over all. Pour cider on, and cover. Cook for 10 or 12 hours on low until meat and vegetables are tender.
15 minutes before serving, check the amount of liquid in the stew. Quite a bit should have accumulated, but add some or all of the water to make as much sauce as desired. Thicken with pureed vegetables and liquid as directed, turning the crockpot to 'high' after returning the vegetable thickener to the mixture.
